en: And you should have seen the look she gave me. I swear, I thought the flesh was going to melt off her face.
ko: 그리고 그녀가 나에게 준 표정을 봤어야 했어.

en: It's not funny. She is not happy unless everyone around her is panic-nauseous or suicidal.
And the clackers just worship her. The who?
ko: 그리고 그 클랙커들은 그녀를 숭배해. 누구?

en: They call them clackers. The sound that their stilettos make in the marble lobby.
It's like clack, clack, clack, clack, clack, clack, clack.
ko: 대리석 로비에서 그들의 스틸레토가 내는 소리는 클락, 클락, 클락, 클락, 클락, 클락, 클락 같아.

en: And they all act like they're curing cancer or something.
ko: 그리고 모두 마치 암을 치료하는 것처럼 행동해.

en: The amount of time and energy that these people spend on these insignificant, minute details.
ko: 이 사람들이 사소한 세부사항에 쏟는 시간과 에너지가 얼마나 많은지.

en: So that tomorrow they can spend another $300,000 reshooting something that was probably fine to begin with
ko: 내일 또 $300,000를 들여 아마 괜찮았던 걸 다시 촬영하기 위해서야.

en: to sell people things they don't need. Good.
ko: 사람들에게 필요 없는 것들을 팔기 위해서.

en: I'm not even hungry anymore. What?
That is why those girls are so skinny. Oh, no, no, no. Give me that.
ko: 그래서 그 여자애들이 그렇게 말라 있는 거야.

en: There's like $8 of Jarlsberg in there.
